title: fix(ux/ai): navbar item to /ai, chat animations, chat history panel + small fixes
body:
## Epic
AI-first design (part 1)

## Problems
* an internal `<Link>` can't have both a tooltip and context menu 
* AI suggestions that show up when clicking a button under input causes centered input to shift down as the suggestions disappear
* AI suggestions had backdrop filter blur and glass bg, but this has poor contrast and is not nice (and is a one-off design!)
![2026-01-07 12 03 46](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ec9d86c1-708e-444b-84ad-208ad12762b8)


## Changes (global)
* Fix `<Link>` to allow both tooltip and context menu
* Fix: keep suggestions in DOM so layout doesn't shift
* Fix: Suggestions background now `bg-surface-primary` for better contrast.
![2026-01-07 12 02 39](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/01473666-6f9b-4e70-92e7-978696421473)



## Changes (all behind flag `AI_UX`)
- Rework DOM/UX chat interface to enable `new <=> threaded chat` animations, when starting new thread... fade intro out, slide down input to bottom, chat messages slide to top for a more cleaner ux. (also work switching chats)
    - new input focus ring color (when chat input is focused, it's purple (like new design), hit enter (start streaming) and it focus ring on chat input goes back to default grey.. when streaming stops, it returns back to purple)
![2026-01-06 19 02 17](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bda8d589-8ec0-4702-87a6-8a9c92050f64)

- `/ai` route gets side panel for chat history + collapsable (new design says it will be a tab, but let's roll with this for now)
![2026-01-06 21 42 55](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/aeff4043-0ab7-4514-96d8-7bd671ffeff8)


- Top level nav item "Posthog AI" (since we'll have it in the new nav design, might to be good to get this in now)
![2026-01-06 18 35 48](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b435969a-690a-422e-a3f1-158d5515fd05)


## How did you test this code?
- non-flagged is the same ✅ 
- flagged is is the new experience ✅ 


## Publish to changelog?
No

P2: Typo: `opacity-1` is not a valid Tailwind CSS class. The opacity scale uses values like `opacity-0`, `opacity-50`, `opacity-100`. This should likely be `starting:opacity-100` for the entry animation to work correctly.</violation>

<violation number="1" location="frontend/src/scenes/max/maxPanelSizingLogic.ts:54">
P1: This listener will never fire. In Kea, `listeners` only respond to actions, but `chatHistoryPanelDesiredSize` is a connected **value** (selector), not an action. To react to size changes, listen to the `chatHistoryPanelSetDesiredSize` action instead (already connected on line 28). Note the action payload format is `{ size }`, not just the value.

title: chore(deps): bump astral-sh/setup-uv from 7.1.6 to 7.2.0
body:
Bumps [astral-sh/setup-uv](https://github.com/astral-sh/setup-uv) from 7.1.6 to 7.2.0.
Release notes

Sourced from astral-sh/setup-uv's releases.

v7.2.0 🌈 add outputs python-version and python-cache-hit

Changes

Among some minor typo fixes and quality of life features for developers of actions the main feature of this release are new outputs:

  • python-version: The Python version that was set (same content as existing UV_PYTHON)
  • python-cache-hit: A boolean value to indicate the Python cache entry was found

While implementing this it became clear, that it is easier to handle the Python binaries in a separate cache entry. The added benefit for users is that the "normal" cache containing the dependencies can be used in all runs no matter if these cache the Python binaries or not.

[!NOTE]
This release will invalidate caches that contain the Python binaries. This happens a single time.

🐛 Bug fixes

title: feat(logs): add log comments
body:
## Problem

Currently, users cannot add comments to logs in the LogsViewer, limiting collaboration and context sharing around log entries.

## Changes

![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/10e4e94a-56d7-47f7-af2a-15b301ded8de.png)

- Added `LOG` to the `ActivityScope` enum in frontend types
- Added `Log` to the activity logging scope list in the backend
- Created a new `LogComments` component to enable commenting on log entries
- Added a Comments tab to the LogDetailsModal
- Updated the `LogDetailsTab` type to include the new 'comments' option

## How did you test this code?

I tested this by:
- Verifying the comments component renders correctly in the LogDetailsModal
- Confirming comments can be added to log entries and are properly associated with the log UUID
- Checking that comment context includes relevant log information (timestamp and service name)
- Ensuring the comments tab navigation works as expected

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: jonmcwest

reviews.body: **No issues found** across 5 files

## Pull request overview This PR adds commenting functionality to log entries in the LogsViewer, enabling users to collaborate and share context around specific log entries. The implementation adds a new "Comments" tab to the LogDetailsModal that leverages the existing comments infrastructure. - Added `LOG` as a new activity scope in both frontend and backend type definitions - Created a new `LogComments` component that integrates the existing comments system - Extended the LogDetailsModal with a new "Comments" tab ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `frontend/src/types.ts` | Added `LOG = 'Log'` to the ActivityScope enum to support commenting on log entries | | `posthog/models/activity_logging/activity_log.py` | Added "Log" to the backend ActivityScope type definition for consistency | | `products/logs/frontend/components/LogsViewer/LogDetailsModal/logDetailsModalLogic.ts` | Extended LogDetailsTab type to include 'comments' option | | `products/logs/frontend/components/LogsViewer/LogDetailsModal/LogDetailsModal.tsx` | Added Comments tab to the modal's tab list with LogComments component | | `products/logs/frontend/components/LogsViewer/LogDetailsModal/LogComments.tsx` | New component that integrates CommentsList and CommentComposer with log-specific context (timestamp and service name) |

title: feat: add new linear integration on replay
body:
## Problem

<!-- Who are we building for, what are their needs, why is this important? -->

<!-- Does this fix an issue? Uncomment the line below with the issue ID to automatically close it when merged -->
<!-- Closes #ISSUE_ID -->
As part of replay everywhere Q1 goal, we'd like to add few integrations onto our replay feature so the user can easily link issues from popular platforms like Linear, Github, Jira etc. Since error tracking already has the setup for the Linear, Github, and Gitlab we'll start from there and then look into adding more integrations for third party apps such as Jira, Trello, Datadog etc depending on usage.

## Changes

<!-- If there are frontend changes, please include screenshots. -->
<!-- If a reference design was involved, include a link to the relevant Figma frame! -->
- Adds a new table `posthog_sessionrecordingexternalreference` to track created and linked issues for Linear for things like title, descriptions, ticket id etc.
- Adds a new ViewSet to handle linear (and other integrations in the future) onto replay backend structure
- New side panel on replay page where the user can create and view linked Linear issues next to the replay video that has been linked to
- Adds a new setting to set up Linear integration with whichever workspace the user has on active and logged in via Oauth, user is prompted to log in if they are not logged into Linear already
- Few snapshot tests and updates on existing tests since the external reference is not added onto replay data from backend send to the frontend
- To start, this new feature will be behind a feature flag `replay-linear-integration` for internal team testing etc.

Recordings of change:

Adding a linear ticket and viewing linked tickets
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8ebb6243-f30d-4e0a-b4f8-7c6c38fd4e04

Adding a new Linear workspace
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f85cc3ee-fa48-452d-a03c-20f1af1f0075

*Things to note: The Linear Oauth is shared across all of posthog, so the user will only have to integrate once in general settings, error tracking settings, or replay settings.


## How did you test this code?

<!-- Briefly describe the steps you took. -->
<!-- Include automated tests if possible, otherwise describe the manual testing routine. -->

<!-- Docs reminder: If this change requires updated docs, please do that! Engineers are the primary people responsible for their documentation. 🙌 -->

👉 _Stay up-to-date with [PostHog coding conventions](https://posthog.com/docs/contribute/coding-conventions) for a smoother review._

Also improved the height styling by adding `h-full` class to ensure the row content properly fills the available space.

Local dev

title: fix(ph-ai): validate mode is in registry
body:
## Problem

We observed a [KeyError in prod](https://us.posthog.com/project/2/error_tracking/019b859d-f3f1-72e1-81af-6fe2fb84f437?timestamp=2026-01-06%2016:39:04.291000-08:00) that could occur when:

  1. conversation state had `agent_mode=ERROR_TRACKING` persisted
  2. the feature flag that controls the mode (if undeer ff) was disabled
  3. look up the mode in `self.mode_registry[self._mode]` with failure

## Changes

* added defensive validation and fallback to our default mode (`PRODUCT_ANALYTICS`)

## How did you test this code?

- [x] unit tests

title: feat(LogsViewer): implement AI-powered log analysis tab in LogDetailsModal
body:
## Problem

Users need better tools to understand and troubleshoot log entries. Currently, they can only view raw log data, which requires manual analysis and interpretation.

## Changes

![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/7c10d756-9a6b-401f-94e0-5c49e2b3fdcb.png)

Added an "Explore with AI" tab to the log details modal that provides AI-powered analysis of log entries:

- Utilises new API endpoint for log analysis with AI
- Added a new tab in the log details modal with AI-powered insights
- Implemented components to display:
  - Severity assessment with visual indicators
  - Impact summary and technical explanation
  - Probable causes with confidence levels
  - Prioritized immediate actions
  - Key fields with significance explanations
- Included proper AI consent handling

## How did you test this code?

- Tested the UI components with various log types and severity levels
- Verified AI consent flow works correctly
- Confirmed filtering functionality works when clicking on key fields
- Tested loading states and error handling
- Verified responsive design in different viewport sizes

## Publish to changelog?

Yes - this is a user-facing feature that provides significant value for log analysis.
author: jonmcwest

title: chore(ci): pin and update all GitHub Actions
body:
Pins all GitHub Actions to commit SHA for supply chain security. Version tags kept as comments for readability.

**Major version bumps** (all Node.js runtime updates, no behavior changes):
- actions/checkout, upload-artifact, download-artifact, cache, github-script
- dorny/paths-filter v2→v3
- datadrivers/terragrunt-action v2→v4 (removed deprecated inputs)

Requires runner v2.327.1+ (GitHub-hosted are fine).

Not pinned: `PostHog/.github` workflow (external repo, uses @main).
author: webjunkie

reviews.body: **No issues found** across 50 files

## Pull request overview

This PR enhances supply chain security by pinning all GitHub Actions to specific commit SHAs while updating to the latest versions. Version tags are preserved as comments for readability. The changes include major version bumps for several core actions (actions/checkout, upload/download-artifact, cache, github-script) and dorny/paths-filter v2→v3, all driven by Node.js runtime updates with no expected behavior changes.

Key changes:
- **Security hardening**: All actions pinned to commit SHAs with version comments
- **Major version updates**: Updated 10+ actions to latest stable versions
- **API changes**: datadrivers/terragrunt-action v2→v4 with parameter name changes

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 50 out of 50 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `.github/workflows/update-bot-ips.yml` | Pins actions/checkout to v6.0.1, peter-evans/create-pull-request to v8.0.0 |
| `.github/workflows/update-ai-costs.yml` | Pins actions/checkout to v6.0.1, pnpm/action-setup to v4.2.0, create-pull-request to v8.0.0 |
| `.github/workflows/terragrunt-posthog.yaml` | Updates actions/checkout to v6.0.1, terragrunt-action to v4.0.3 with parameter changes |
| `.github/workflows/stale.yaml` | Updates actions/stale to v10.1.1 |
| `.github/workflows/shellcheck.yml` | Pins actions/checkout to v6.0.1 |
| `.github/workflows/rust-docker-build.yml` | Updates multiple Docker/Depot actions and repository-dispatch to v4.0.1 |
| `.github/workflows/release.yml` | Pins upload/download-artifact to v6.0.0/v7.0.0, checkout to v6.0.1 |
| `.github/workflows/pr-updated.yml` | Updates amannn/action-semantic-pull-request to v6.1.1 |
| `.github/workflows/pr-preview-deploy.yml` | Updates Docker actions and aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials to v5.1.1 |
| `.github/workflows/pr-preview-cleanup.yml` | Pins aws-actions to latest versions |
| `.

Can we do a couple changes? Left them as comments  
  
Also, we might want a script on `bin/`to update these. Also, did you guarantee all of these new hashes are actually safe and don't have any bad CVEs?
This is slightly difficult/tedious to review but seems reasonable enough. Will our use of `@main` for `PostHog/.github` prevent us from enabling the repo setting to require all actions to be pinned?
Approving because tom's reviewed it :)

```text

<violation number="1">
P0: This change unpins `actions/checkout` from a commit SHA to a version tag, which is a security regression. The PR description claims to "pin all GitHub Actions to commit SHA for supply chain security" but this does the opposite.

This is especially critical because:
1. This workflow uses `pull_request_target` which bypasses security checks
2. Version tags like `v6` can be moved/compromised; SHAs are immutable
3. The action executes before checkout, so the `ref: master` safeguard doesn't protect against a compromised action

Keep the SHA pin and add the version comment for readability.</violation>

title: feat(temporal/metrics): Enable dual metric emission in temporal
body: ## Problem Whilst working on a refactor for the metrics emitted in our subscription / export flow (PR: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/44247), I noticed that we currently only emit `Temporal` metrics within the temporal workers. E.g. code needs to explicitly export metrics through the Temporal metric runner in order for metrics to be exported. This however means that: - Code using the "django flow" (e.g. celery or the main app) will not emit Temporal metrics (acceptable as Temporal is a subflow) - Code using the "temporal flow" will not emit any PromQL metrics exported by the Django flow (unacceptable, this leaves a gap in our observability) The reason for this is that the Temporal workers use their own PromQLRegistry, which excludes / ignores any metrics emitted from the main django flow unless they use the specific Temporal `get_metric_meter()` function. ([Slack thread with more context](https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C09BDQLM8KA/p1767622989159989)) ## Changes This PR introduces a "dual metric emittence" solution for the metrics within the Temporal workers. Effectively it merges the metrics from Temporal and PromQL into a single unified view, resulting in a `/metrics` endpoint which returns both temporal and promql metrics. I've gone back and forth on the implementation, and the following segment is only relevant if you want a bit of the architectural scope of the solution: So after some research, it turned out there were a total of 6 options: | Option | Approach | Pros | Cons | |-------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1. Shared Registry | Have Temporal SDK use prometheus_client.REGISTRY directly | Single source of truth | Not possible - Temporal SDK uses internal Rust-based metrics system | | 2. HTTP Fetching ✅ | Expose Temporal metrics on internal port, fetch and merge | Preserves exact Temporal format, simple implementation | Extra HTTP call per scrape (few ms latency) | | 3. MetricBuffer Interception | Capture Temporal metrics via buffer, convert to prometheus_client | Direct in-memory access | Format incompatibilities (see below) | | 4. Custom Prometheus Exporter | Write custom exporter handling both metric sources | Full control | High complexity, maintenance burden | | 5. Migrate to OpenTelemetry | Switch entire metrics stack to OTEL (Temporal supports it natively) | Modern standard, unified metrics pipeline | Large migration effort, requires changing all existing prometheus_client usage | | 6. Separate Endpoints | Expose two endpoints, configure Prometheus to scrape both | No code changes needed | Requires Helm/infra changes, two scrape configs to maintain | Initially we looked at option 5, however this is such a massive migration for, currently, little to none added value. Whilst it would be nice to be vendor agnostic, I don't see a situation in the near future where we would move all of our metrics away from Prometheus, and as such, new metrics likely would still be added in the Prometheus format (instead of the OTEL format). Option 6 was dropped as it would complicate our helm-charts for our temporal workers as we would need to add a new scrape target (making use of a different helm chart / operator in order to facilitate this) + it would not be clear from an application PoV which metrics are going where. __Why `MetricBuffer` Didn't Work__ We initially attempted Option 3 using Temporal's MetricBuffer to intercept metrics and convert them to `prometheus_client` format. This ran into several incompatibilities: 1. Counter `_total` suffix - `prometheus_client.Counter` hardcodes the `_total` suffix in `generate_latest()`. Temporal emits counters without this suffix (e.g., `temporal_request` not `temporal_request_total`), causing TYPE/name mismatches that Prometheus rejects. 2. Label validation - Python's `prometheus_client` enforces strict label name validation at metric creation time. Temporal's metric labels don't always conform to these rules, causing runtime errors when trying to create equivalent `prometheus_client` metrics ([code for this can be found here](https://github.com/prometheus/client_python/blob/e8f8bae6554de11ebffffcc878ab19abd67528f2/prometheus_client/metrics.py#L175) and [docs](https://prometheus.io/docs/instrumenting/writing_clientlibs/#labels)). - [Docs](https://python.temporal.io/temporalio.runtime.TelemetryConfig.html) - [Someone in a similar situation](https://community.temporal.io/t/merging-prometheus-metrics-from-temporal-sdk-and-local-services-in-a-python-application/12858) __Chosen Solution: HTTP Fetching__ We therefore opted for Option 2 - exposing Temporal SDK metrics on an internal localhost port via `PrometheusConfig`, then fetching and merging with `prometheus_client metrics`. This: - Preserves Temporal's exact metric format (no conversion needed) - Gracefully degrades if Temporal endpoint is unavailable - Adds minimal overhead (localhost HTTP call, which gets called every scrape interval, thus roughly every ~15-30s) ## How did you test this code? ✅ New tests are passing ✅ All metrics from Temporal are present in the new output: 1. Switch to `master` and navigate towards http://localhost:8001/metrics, saving the output as "old.txt" 2. Switch to this branch and navigate towards http://localhost:8001/metrics ,saving the output as "new.txt" 3. Run: `comm -23 <(grep -v '^#' old.txt | grep -v '^$' | sed 's/ [^ ]*$//' | sort -u) \ <(grep -v '^#' new.txt | grep -v '^$' | sed 's/ [^ ]*$//' | sort -u)` (ignoring the values, but checking if labels match as well) The diff here is accepted / fine as that is a uniquely generated ID, the rest all matches which confirms this is working as expected. ✅ Running the subscription flow, correctly increments the `django promql` metrics ✅ Passing the metric output through `promtool` validates as expected: ``` ❯ curl -s http://localhost:8001/metrics | promtool check metrics cohort_staleness_hours use base unit "seconds" instead of "hours" cohort_stuck_count non-histogram and non-summary metrics should not have "_count" suffix posthog_redis_could_not_decompress_value_counter_total metric name should not include type 'counter' posthog_session_summary_tokens_in_prompt_histogram metric name should not include type 'histogram' posthog_survey_summary_tokens_in_prompt_histogram metric name should not include type 'histogram' replay_playlists_in_redis_gauge metric name should not include type 'gauge' replay_total_playlists_gauge metric name should not include type 'gauge' session_snapshots_generate_pre_signed_url_histogram metric name should not include type 'histogram' session_snapshots_loading_v2_lts_counter_total metric name should not include type 'counter' subscription_queued counter metrics should have "_total" suffix subscription_send_failure counter metrics should have "_total" suffix subscription_send_success counter metrics should have "_total" suffix temporal_activity_poll_no_task counter metrics should have "_total" suffix temporal_activity_task_received counter metrics should have "_total" suffix temporal_long_request counter metrics should have "_total" suffix temporal_request counter metrics should have "_total" suffix temporal_sticky_cache_hit counter metrics should have "_total" suffix temporal_worker_start counter metrics should have "_total" suffix temporal_workflow_completed counter metrics should have "_total" suffix temporal_workflow_task_queue_poll_empty counter metrics should have "_total" suffix temporal_workflow_task_queue_poll_succeed counter metrics should have "_total" suffix ``` All these warnings are "fine" and are expected. - The "seconds instead of hours" is a naming thing, which is not a regression introduced here. - The "metrics should not have '_count' suffix" is a naming thing, which is not a regression introduced here. - The "metric name should not include type 'xxx'" is a naming thing, which is not a regression introduced here (and you can find them as is in the code definition). - The "metrics should have '_total' suffix" is the result of how temporal manages their counters where it omits the "_total" suffix, which is not a regression introduced here. All in all, prometheus parsed the metrics fine and it just flagged some metrics which might not adhere to their best practices which is acceptable. 👉 _Stay up-to-date with [PostHog coding conventions](https://posthog.com/docs/contribute/coding-conventions) for a smoother review._ ## Publish to changelog? No

title: chore(exports): Fix a bug where single exports incorrectly mis-report failed exports (leading to uncaught exceptions in our monitoring)
body: ## Problem Whilst working on improving our error tracking regarding the different possible failures around the `ExportAsset` (through both the "single" UI export and the subscription export flow), I noticed that in the first case (the single UI export), we have a code path where we do not set the `exception` and `exception_type` fields upon a retryable exception (even during the last attempt). This does not match with our logic inside the subscription export flow, and in the near future, when we will split the exceptions in different failure categories, it will cause us to be blindsided to retryable errors happening in this code path. As it's crucial that we're informed on all different failure paths (so that we can observe regressions and act upon them), we have aligned the error capture model between both flows so that we cover all different paths. This will also help in reducing the number of "uncaught exceptions" we are seeing in our dashboards, further limiting those to only contain timeouts. ## Changes 1. Effectively we now also log `exception` and `exception_type` details upon retryable failures inside the "single UI export flow". 1. In order to enable this, I had to move the `transaction.atomic` annotation from a decorator to a function call, as otherwise we would still not correctly set the failures during failures (as it would roll back the resource). 2. Additionally I've centralised the logic to record failures, so that we can guarantee that both flows stay in sync. ## How did you test this code? 1. I've added a test case to cover the regression `test_retriable_error_records_failure_on_final_attempt` and confirmed it failed with the code from master. 2. All added tests pass. 3. Manual export still works: [export-weekly-active-users-waus.csv](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24432935/export-weekly-active-users-waus.csv) 4. Subscriptions still work: 👉 _Stay up-to-date with [PostHog coding conventions](https://posthog.com/docs/contribute/coding-conventions) for a smoother review._ ## Publish to changelog? No

We have a couple of legacy API endpoints. Users still seem to be able to find these somewhere, and usage is increasing. I tried locating where users find out about the endpoints, but can't find anything.

Assuming it's projects like this https://github.com/kamranayub/dotposthog and LLMs.

title: feat: Add new AI-based usecase onboarding
body:
This PRs does a handful of things to our onboarding:

- Looks at what docs pages we've accessed on the website and uses that as the default selection of products when people choose "Pick myself"
- Made the list of usecases more pleasant to the eye by using three columns in wider screens
- Simplified the `urls` object by having all go through a single `urls.onboarding()` helper

And then, more importantly, hidden by a feature flag, we have a new AI-based use case onboarding where we ask people what they expect from PostHog and we then suggest products based on that.
We'll run this change behind an experiment, of course, since people might not like having an AI there vs. just picking a use case themselves.



![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/7aa7ef3a-5f48-49a1-9d55-92ca12dae00e.png)

![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/d001ea5d-4cdb-410e-86bb-e1ccd2eee2fd.png)
author: rafaeelaudibert

<file name="frontend/src/scenes/onboarding/productSelection/productSelectionLogic.ts">

<violation number="1" location="frontend/src/scenes/onboarding/productSelection/productSelectionLogic.ts:190">
P2: Duplicate `productKeyMap` definition. This same mapping is defined again in the `submitAiRecommendationSuccess` listener below (lines 241-250). Consider extracting this to a shared constant at the module level to avoid duplication and potential inconsistencies if products are added/changed.</violation>
</file>

<file name="frontend/src/scenes/billing/PlanComparison.tsx">

<violation number="1" location="frontend/src/scenes/billing/PlanComparison.tsx:14">
P2: Function signature mismatch after import change: `getProductIcon` now expects `(iconColor, iconKey, iconSize)` but this call passes `(name, icon_key, className)`. The first argument `includedProduct.name` will be interpreted as a color, not the product name. Either update this call to match the new signature, or the function should be called with `includedProduct.icon_key` as the color parameter (likely `undefined` if no color is needed).</violation>
</file>

<file name="frontend/src/scenes/onboarding/onboardingLogic.tsx">

<violation number="1" location="frontend/src/scenes/onboarding/onboardingLogic.tsx:414">
P1: Calling `setProductKey(null)` triggers the listener that redirects to `urls.default()`, which will immediately navigate users away from the onboarding page instead of allowing them to re-select a product. Consider using the reducer directly via a separate action that only clears the state without triggering the redirect, or modify the listener to handle this case.</violation>

title: feat(surveys): add AI summarization for open-ended responses
body:
## Summary

This PR adds always-on AI summarization for survey open-ended responses, building on top of #44257 (layout changes).

**Key features:**

- **Auto-trigger** - Summarization starts automatically when 10+ responses exist
- **Smart caching** - Re-summarizes only when 5+ new responses arrive since last summary
- **Google Gemini** - Uses `gemini-3-flash-preview` model with structured Pydantic output
- **Server-side cache** - Summaries stored in `question_summaries` JSONField on Survey model

## Changes

### Backend (`products/surveys/backend/summarization/`)

- New summarization module following `llm_analytics` pattern
- `models.py` - GeminiModel enum
- `constants.py` - Default model configuration
- `fetch.py` - HogQL query to fetch responses for summarization
- `formatting.py` - Markdown formatting with frequency indicators (🔥 common, 📊 moderate, 💡 rare)
- `llm/schema.py` - Pydantic models for structured output (SurveySummaryResponse, SurveyTheme)
- `llm/gemini.py` - Gemini API integration with structured output

### Backend (API)

- `posthog/api/survey.py` - Updated `summarize_responses` endpoint with caching logic
- `posthog/models/surveys/survey.py` - Added `question_summaries` JSONField

### Frontend

- `OpenQuestionSummaryV2.tsx` - New component showing inline AI summary with:
    - Loading skeleton while generating
    - Error state with retry button
    - Regenerate button for manual refresh
    - Response count and generation timestamp
- `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` - Updated API types for summary response

![CleanShot 2026-01-05 at 12.04.28@2x.jpg](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/da2b9ce1-2729-42f5-a6bd-714c0fed3698.jpg)
author: lucasheriques

reviews.body:
<sub>13 files reviewed, 6 comments</sub>

<sub>[Edit Code Review Agent Settings](https://app.greptile.com/review/github) | [Greptile](https://greptile.com?utm_source=greptile_expert&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=code_reviews&utm_content=posthog_44258)</sub>

**2 issues found** across 13 files

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents (all issues)</summary>

```text

Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them.


<file name="posthog/api/survey.py">

<violation number="1" location="posthog/api/survey.py:1474">
P1: Missing smart caching logic: The PR description states "Re-summarizes only when 5+ new responses arrive since last summary", but this feature is not implemented. The code stores `responseCount` in the cached summary but never compares it with the current response count before returning cached data. Consider fetching the current response count and comparing with `cached_summary.get("responseCount", 0)` to determine if regeneration is needed.</violation>
</file>

<file name="posthog/models/surveys/survey.py">

<violation number="1" location="posthog/models/surveys/survey.py:248">
P1: Adding a new field to a Django model requires a database migration. Run `python manage.py makemigrations` to generate the migration for the new `question_summaries` JSONField, otherwise this field won't exist in the database schema.</violation>
</file>
```

</details>

<sub>Reply with feedback, questions, or to request a fix. Tag `@cubic-dev-ai` to re-run a review.</sub>
i think my only "blocking" comments are using the posthog wrapper to get LLM analytics & making sure we can map feedback events to the specific llm generations, but otherwise looks great!

looks great!! left one small comment, and would like to hear your thoughts on these things, but nothing blocking:

1. sampling/randomizing/ordering responses for the summary -- looks like the query does not have an explicit order right now, should we do either most recent N or randomize them? and then in the UI, say "based on last 100 responses" or "based on a random sample of 100 responses"? OR, if we want to strictly enforce a limit, just give the user a toggle "use latest responses -- sample random responses"
2. LLM citing sources, and linking to them - maybe we can do this in a followup if you agree?
3. what's the latency on this endpoint? i guess we can monitor prod traffic for more realistic insights, but we might want to transition to a streaming approach for better perceived latency if this is loading on page-load or the user clicks refresh

commits.message:
feat: add AI summarization for survey open-ended responses

- Add OpenQuestionSummaryV2 component with auto-trigger at 10+ responses
- Re-summarize when 5+ new responses arrive since last summary
- Use Google Gemini (gemini-3-flash-preview) for structured LLM output
- Add server-side caching in question_summaries JSONField on Survey model
- Create products/surveys/backend/summarization/ module following llm_analytics pattern
- Add API endpoint for fetching/generating summaries with caching
- Show loading skeleton, error states, and regenerate button in UI

refactor: use singleton Gemini client to avoid re-instantiation

Use module-level lazy singleton pattern for the Gemini client
to avoid creating a new client instance on each API call.

fix: address PR feedback for survey AI summarization

- Remove emojis from AI formatting (replaces 🔥/📊/💡 with text labels)
- Add explicit limits to prompt (max 5 themes, 1-2 sentence overview)
- Add PostHog LLM analytics wrapper with trace_id for feedback mapping
- Make summary collapsible to save vertical space
- Add sampling warning when not all responses are analyzed
- Add AI disclaimer to generated summaries

fix: add accuracy disclaimer to AI summary

Aligns with Max's "can make mistakes" pattern to set proper expectations
chore: rename migration to 0959 to avoid conflict with master
fix: soften AI disclaimer wording per review feedback
fix: restore 0956 migration to match master
fix: use proper GeminiClient type and add trace_id to API response type
fix: create Gemini client per-request to ensure default_client is available
fix: enable posthoganalytics in dev mode for LLM analytics, create client per-request
fix: remove genai.Client type annotation to fix mypy error

title: feat: Disallow modifying unused sensitive fields from Admin
body:
## Problem

Django's admin models take the approach of allowing staff to modify all fields by default. But there are sensitive fields that shouldn't be modified outside of the standard user flow.

## Changes

Staff can no longer perform the following sensitive actions:

- Modify a user's email, pending email, or email verification status
- Modify a domain's domain or verification status
- Add a new domain to an organization
- Modify a member's organization (i.e. move a member between organizations)
- Modify a user's existing TOTP MFA device, or add a new one

title: feat(workflows): nodejs changes to support batch workflows
body: ## Problem PostHog workflows users want the ability to send broadcasts, or more generally for workflows use case, trigger N workflow invocations for persons based on some criteria they define. ## Changes Here's a small system diagram explaining what's going on here (open in new tab to see full res) ## How did you test this code? - Added or adding tests for every new bit of functionality - Tested everything e2e locally - Will test in dev, then on team 2 once charts changes are landed
author: havenbarnes

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR introduces batch workflow capabilities to PostHog, enabling users to trigger workflow invocations for multiple persons matching specific property criteria. The implementation adds a new Kafka-based consumer that processes batch workflow requests, queries persons from the database using property filters, and creates individual workflow invocations for each matching person.

- Adds property-based person querying with support for multiple filter operators (exact, regex, date comparisons, etc.)
- Implements a new consumer (`CdpBatchHogFlowRequestsConsumer`) to process batch workflow requests from Kafka
- Introduces a new API endpoint for triggering batch workflow invocations

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 14 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated 25 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| nodejs/src/worker/ingestion/persons/repositories/raw-postgres-person-repository.ts | Adds interface methods for counting and fetching persons by property filters |
| nodejs/src/worker/ingestion/persons/repositories/postgres-person-repository.ts | Implements property filter querying logic with SQL query construction for multiple operators |
| nodejs/src/worker/ingestion/persons/repositories/person-repository.ts | Adds interface methods for batch person operations |
| nodejs/src/types.ts | Adds new plugin server mode and configuration fields for batch workflow processing |
| nodejs/src/server.ts | Integrates the batch hogflow consumer into the server startup |
| nodejs/src/config/kafka-topics.ts | Defines new Kafka topic for batch hogflow requests |
| nodejs/src/config/config.ts | Adds configuration defaults for batch workflow processing |
| nodejs/src/cdp/utils.ts | Adds utility function to create invocation globals for batch workflows |
| nodejs/src/cdp/services/managers/persons-manager.service.ts | Implements streaming pagination for batch p

</file>

P0: SQL injection vulnerability: `key` from user input is directly interpolated into SQL. Use `sanitizeSqlIdentifier(key)` (already imported) to sanitize the property key before interpolation, e.g., `posthog_person.properties->>'${sanitizeSqlIdentifier(key)}'`.</violation>

title: fix(temporal): disable local variable dumping in exception logs
body:
## Problem

Temporal worker exception logs can dump local variables via structlog's `ExceptionRenderer`, which by default has `show_locals=True`. This causes:

1. **Security concerns**: locals will be logged in plaintext when exceptions occur
2. **Log bloat**: Exception stack traces are already ~16KB per line, contributing to promtail OOMs on temporal worker nodes. this should help reduce these events

## Changes

- Replace `structlog.processors.dict_tracebacks` with explicit `ExceptionRenderer(ExceptionDictTransformer(show_locals=False))`
- Preserves JSON-formatted exception tracebacks (type, value, file, line, function)
- Stops dumping local variables at each stack frame

## How did you test this code?

Local test comparing output with `show_locals=True` vs `show_locals=False`:

Existing test passes:
```
pytest posthog/temporal/tests/common/test_logger.py::test_logger_renders_tracebacks -v
# PASSED
```

## Publish to changelog?

no
author: bciaraldi

reviews.body: **No issues found** across 1 file

commits.message: fix(temporal): disable local variable dumping in exception logs
Merge branch 'bc/fix-temporal-show-locals' of github.com:PostHog/posthog into bc/fix-temporal-show-locals

title: fix: ensure that filter buttons shows in ActionFilterRow
body:
## Problem

The filter button (property filters icon) disappeared in the ActionFilter component after https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/42551  when the feature flag PRODUCT_ANALYTICS_EVENTS_COMBINATION_IN_TRENDS is off.

## Changes

In ActionFilterRow.tsx:629, the entire end section containing the filter button is conditionally rendered based on rowEndElements.length. The commit changed enablePopup to be true for trends contexts, which removed propertyFiltersButton from rowEndElements. When all hide options are also true (like in DashboardTemplateVariables.tsx), and showCombine is false (feature flag off), rowEndElements becomes empty, causing the entire section (including the filter button rendered separately inside) to not render.

Changed the condition at ActionFilterRow.tsx:629 from:
  {rowEndElements.length ? (
  to:
  {rowEndElements.length ||
  ((mathAvailability === MathAvailability.FunnelsOnly || isTrendsContext) && !hideFilter) ? (

  This ensures that when we're in trends/funnels context and hideFilter is false, the end section renders regardless of rowEndElements being empty, allowing the filter button to appear via the separate {!hideFilter && propertyFiltersButton} render inside.

## How did you test this code?

Tested locally.

## Pull request overview

This PR adds support for batch workflows in PostHog, enabling users to trigger N workflow invocations for persons based on defined criteria (broadcasts). The implementation spans frontend TypeScript changes for triggering batch workflows and backend Django changes for managing batch job state and communicating with the plugin server.

Key changes:
- New `HogFlowBatchJob` model to track batch workflow execution status
- Frontend logic updated to handle batch workflow triggering separately from manual triggers
- API endpoint added for creating batch workflow jobs

title: fix: Impersonation expiry redirects to wrong user's admin page
body:
## Problem

  After an impersonation session expired, navigating to a different user's admin page (e.g., `/admin/posthog/user/456/change/`) would incorrectly redirect to the originally impersonated user's admin page (e.g., `/admin/posthog/user/123/change/`) instead of the intended destination.

## Steps to reproduce:
  1. Log in as a user via impersonation
  2. Close the tab
  3. Wait for the impersonation session to expire (30 min idle timeout)
  4. Open a new tab and navigate directly to a different user's admin page
  5. Get incorrectly redirected to the original impersonated user's admin page

## Root cause

Commit 223aa62 introduced a logout handler in AutoLogoutImpersonateMiddleware that captured the impersonated user's PK and redirected to their admin page. This handler ran for all /logout requests, including those triggered by session expiry.

## New behavior

  | Scenario                                       | Redirect destination           |
  |------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|
  | Session expires on non-admin page              | /admin/                        |
  | Session expires while navigating to admin page | Intended admin page            |
  | Explicit logout (clicking logout button)       | Impersonated user's admin page |

Tests have been updated/added for all three scenarios.

commits.message:
fix: Impersonation expiry redirects to wrong user's admin page

When an impersonation session expired while navigating to a different user's admin page, users were incorrectly redirected to the originally impersonated user's page instead of their intended destination.

Now session expiry redirects to the intended admin page (or /admin/), while explicit logout still redirects to the impersonated user's page.

title: fix: Use kw-only formatter when preparing query
body:
## Problem

When preparing a query using `ClickHouseClient` we still sometimes rely on formatting query parameters into the query. Notably, this is used for filters. The problem is that filters can contain values like `{2}` which are interpreted by `str.format` as a positional argument index, and we don't pass any positional arguments when formatting (because there are none to pass).

<!-- Who are we building for, what are their needs, why is this important? -->

<!-- Does this fix an issue? Uncomment the line below with the issue ID to automatically close it when merged -->
<!-- Closes #ISSUE_ID -->

## Changes

Switch to a new keyword only formatter that returns a numerical index unchanged. 

<!-- If there are frontend changes, please include screenshots. -->
<!-- If a reference design was involved, include a link to the relevant Figma frame! -->

Manually verified first, then added a new unit test. Unit test fails on `master`, but passes here.

This change introduces a new tool to facilitate this. ## Changes Adds a new MCP tool `dashboard-reorder-tiles` that allows users to reorder tiles on a specified dashboard. - **`services/mcp/typescript/src/tools/dashboards/reorderTiles.ts`**: New tool handler for reordering dashboard tiles. - **`services/mcp/typescript/src/api/client.ts`**: Adds a `reorderTiles` API method that updates tile layouts based on the provided order. - **`services/mcp/typescript/src/schema/dashboards.ts` & `services/mcp/typescript/src/schema/tool-inputs.ts`**: New schema `ReorderDashboardTilesSchema` for input validation. - **`services/mcp/schema/tool-definitions.json`**: Adds `dashboard-reorder-tiles` tool definition. - **`services/mcp/typescript/src/tools/index.ts`**: Registers the new tool. - **`services/mcp/typescript/tests/tools/dashboards.integration.test.ts`**: Adds an integration test for the reorder tiles functionality. - **`services/mcp/typescript/tests/unit/tool-filtering.test.ts` & `tests/integration/feature-routing.test.ts`**: Updates to include the new tool in dashboard tool expectations. ## How did you test this code? - Added a dedicated integration test (`services/mcp/typescript/tests/tools/dashboards.integration.test.ts`) that creates a dashboard, adds insights, reorders them, and verifies the success of the operation. ## Publish to changelog? Yes --- [Slack Thread](https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C011L071P8U/p1767366539285079?thread_ts=1767366539.285079&cid=C011L071P8U)

## Pull request overview This PR removes temporary code that was previously excluding duplicate resource attributes from log attributes results. The exclusion logic was a workaround during a transition period when resource attributes were being written to both `resource_attributes` and `attributes` fields. Since the transition is complete, this code is no longer necessary. - Removed the `exclude_expression` logic and associated subquery that filtered out resource attributes from log attributes - Simplified the SQL query by removing the unnecessary exclusion condition - Added a return type annotation to the `where()` method for consistency ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `products/logs/backend/log_attributes_query_runner.py` | Removed temporary exclusion logic that prevented duplicate resource attributes from appearing in log attributes results, along with the associated subquery and placeholder | | `products/logs/backend/logs_query_runner.py` | Added return type annotation `-> ast.Expr` to the `where()` method for type consistency | The changes look clean and appropriate. The removal of the exclusion logic is complete, including: - The conditional `exclude_expression` variable and its complex subquery - The reference to `{exclude_expression}` in the main SQL query - The placeholder in the query parameters The type annotation addition in `logs_query_runner.py` brings consistency with the same method in `log_attributes_query_runner.py`, which already has this annotation. No issues were found with these changes. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.

title: feat(data-warehouse): Move towards using S3 table functions without access keys
body:
## Problem
- We explicitly add aws access key/secret to all S3 queries to our dwh bucket - there is a risk of accidentally leaking these credentials 

## Changes
- Clickhouse now supports access to our dwh S3 bucket without explicit access keys, so we no longer need to add them into the Clickhouse queries, reducing the risk of the keys getting leaked via error messages
- Local dev will still use an access key/secret, but in cloud/prod, it'll be keyless 

## How did you test this code?
- Tested locally and added a unit test
author: Gilbert09

reviews.body: **No issues found** across 2 files

## Pull request overview This PR enhances security by enabling keyless S3 access for ClickHouse queries in production environments, reducing the risk of credential leakage through error messages or logs. **Key changes:** - Modified credential handling logic to only use explicit access keys from settings in DEBUG or TEST modes - Production environments now leverage ClickHouse's built-in credential provider chain (IAM roles) for S3 access - Added test coverage for the new keyless production behavior ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `posthog/hogql/database/s3_table.py` | Updated `build_function_call` to conditionally populate access keys only in DEBUG/TEST modes, enabling keyless S3 access in production | | `posthog/hogql/database/test/test_s3_table.py` | Added test case validating that S3 queries omit credentials when DEBUG and TEST are both disabled | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message: Move towards using S3 table functions without access keys

title: fix(data-warehouse): Nullify the credential IDs for mat view tables and dont add new creds
body:
## Problem
- Follow on from https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/44274 but for mat views 

## Changes
- Adds checks to mat views (and a missing test for the source pipeline) to make sure no new credential rows are added for new sources and mat views
- Removes the credential FK from warehouse tables for mat views, so that `S3Table` fallsback onto the default keys for our bucket instead
  - I was gonna delete the credential rows, but they _somehow_ didn't get hard deleted in my last PR, and so we now have 50 million orphaned rows. This will only add an extra few thousand to the mix - a worthy trade off for a much simpler migration. These orphaned rows can get cleaned up later on 

## How did you test this code?
Tests
author: Gilbert09

reviews.body: **No issues found** across 6 files

## Pull request overview This PR is a follow-up to #44274 that extends credential removal to materialized view tables. It ensures that materialized view tables no longer use dedicated credentials and instead fall back to default S3 bucket credentials. **Key changes:** - Migration to nullify credential foreign keys for all materialized view tables - Removal of credential creation and assignment logic in the table creation workflow - Addition of test assertions to verify credentials are not set ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | products/data_warehouse/backend/migrations/0014_mat_view_credential_deletion.py | Adds migration to set credential_id to NULL for all tables referenced by saved queries (materialized views) | | products/data_warehouse/backend/migrations/test/test_migration_0014.py | Comprehensive test suite for the migration covering various scenarios with materialized and regular tables | | products/data_warehouse/backend/migrations/max_migration.txt | Updates the max migration pointer to the new migration | | products/data_warehouse/backend/data_load/create_table.py | Removes credential creation and assignment logic from the materialized view table creation workflow | | posthog/temporal/tests/data_modeling/test_run_workflow.py | Adds assertion to verify materialized view tables have no credential_id set | | posthog/temporal/tests/data_imports/test_end_to_end.py | Adds assertion to verify source tables have no credential_id set |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.

title: feat(ph-ai): search error tracking tool
body:
## Problem

PostHog AI's error tracking integration was limited and lacked proper context awareness. When users asked about explaining the errors the user was visiting, we were failing. Even the `MaxTool` we had for `Explain This` was not working.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd4c7ffa-c4b1-4070-8638-06d656011faa

This meant users had to manually navigate to error tracking pages and couldn't leverage PostHog AI for error investigation workflows E2E.

## Changes

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9a0a0cea-34be-4d10-9ad8-670167bf82b3

(this shows the E2E work after all 3 stacked PRs are combined)

* Implements the error tracking issues search tool, so that users can correctly search and filter error tracking issues by status, date range, frequency, and text queries.
* Added `ErrorTracking` agent mode

The previous approach would have teh base `search` tool call with free-text string whilst now we are going to use a structured `ErrorTrackingQuery` schema so that we piggyback on existing Error Tracking UI work.

## How did you test this code?

- [x] unit tests
- [x] manual tests

**Note**: Work is splitted into three stacked PRs:

1. https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/44050 (this one)
2. https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/44055
3. https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/44057

body:
## Problem

The logs viewer currently lacks a breakdown option for the sparkline visualization, limiting users' ability to analyze log data by different dimensions.

## Changes

- Added a breakdown selector to the logs sparkline visualization, allowing users to view logs data by either severity or service
- Implemented the necessary logic to fetch and display sparkline data based on the selected breakdown
- Added appropriate color coding for different breakdown options

## How did you test this code?

- Tested switching between different breakdown options (severity and service) and verified the sparkline updates correctly
- Verified that the color coding works appropriately for each breakdown type
- Checked that the sparkline data is properly fetched and displayed when changing the breakdown option
- Ensured that the live tail feature still works correctly with the new breakdown functionality

title: feat(logs-sparkline): add LogsSparklineBreakdownBy to breakdown sparkline by severity and service name
body:
## Problem

Currently, the logs sparkline endpoint doesn't support breaking down data by different fields, limiting the visualization options for log data.

## Changes

- Added a new `sparklineBreakdownBy` field to the `LogsQuery` interface that allows specifying how to break down sparkline data
- Created a new `LogsSparklineBreakdownBy` enum type with options for 'severity' and 'service'
- Updated the sparkline query runner to use the specified breakdown field
- Modified the response format to dynamically include the breakdown field in the results
- Added schema definitions in both TypeScript and Python

## How did you test this code?

I tested this by:
- Verifying the schema changes are consistent across frontend and backend
- Running the sparkline endpoint with different breakdown values
- Checking that the response format correctly includes the breakdown field
- Ensuring backward compatibility with existing code

No

title: feat(Sparkline): add tooltipRowCutoff prop to limit series in tooltip
body:
## Problem

The Sparkline component currently doesn't allow customization of the number of series shown in the tooltip, which means that we can't show more than 8 series - which is needed for the logs sparkline.

## Changes

Added a new optional prop `tooltipRowCutoff` to the Sparkline component that allows limiting the number of series displayed in the tooltip. The default value is set to 8 rows.

- Added TypeScript prop definition with JSDoc comment
- Added the prop to the component's parameter list
- Passed the value to the tooltip component via the `rowCutoff` prop

## How did you test this code?

Tested by rendering Sparkline components with various numbers of data series and verifying that:
- The tooltip correctly limits displayed rows to the specified cutoff value
- The default behavior (8 rows) works as expected when no value is provided
- The component continues to function properly with all other existing features

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: jonmcwest

reviews.body: **No issues found** across 1 file

## Pull request overview This PR adds a `tooltipRowCutoff` prop to the Sparkline component, allowing consumers to customize the maximum number of series displayed in the tooltip. This addresses the need to show more than the default 8 series in scenarios like the logs sparkline. **Key changes:** - Added optional `tooltipRowCutoff` prop to SparklineProps interface with JSDoc documentation - Wired the prop through to the InsightTooltip component's `rowCutoff` parameter --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message: feat(Sparkline): add tooltipRowCutoff prop to limit series in tooltip

title: chore(data-warehouse): Cleanup credentials for managed sources
body:
## Problem
- We create a new `DataWarehouseCredential` model for every table from warehouse sources, this means when we rotate S3 credentials, we have to update millions of rows in the DB (via encrypted fields - not easy!)

## Changes
- Dont create a credential row for each table from a managed source
- `DataWarehouseCredential` only gets used by self-managed sources
- Delete all the existing `DataWarehouseCredential` rows for managed sources and update the FK on the `DataWarehouseTable` model
- Use the env var S3 credentials as the default for managed sources
  - **Note**: this is gonna change very soon (tomorrow) to use role-based access from clickhouse instead

## How did you test this code?
- Added tests for the migration
- Everything else is covered by existing tests
author: Gilbert09

reviews.body:
**3 issues found** across 6 files

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents (all issues)</summary>

```text

Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them.


<file name="products/data_warehouse/backend/migrations/test/test_migration_0013.py">

<violation number="1" location="products/data_warehouse/backend/migrations/test/test_migration_0013.py:88">
P2: Misleading comment - no table actually uses this credential and the description is contradictory (a table can't 'use' a credential if credential_id is NULL). Consider updating to clarify the actual test scenario, e.g., 'Not referenced by any table with external_data_source_id'.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="products/data_warehouse/backend/migrations/test/test_migration_0013.py:208">
P2: Test data `to_keep_2` is created but never explicitly tested. The parameterized test only checks `to_keep_1` and `unused`. Consider adding `("to_keep_2",)` to ensure this credential is properly verified.</violation>
</file>

<file name="products/data_warehouse/backend/migrations/0013_credential_deletion.py">

<violation number="1" location="products/data_warehouse/backend/migrations/0013_credential_deletion.py:14">
P1: Remove explicit `BEGIN;` and `COMMIT;` statements. Django migrations are atomic by default, so the migration is already wrapped in a transaction. Explicit transaction control inside `RunSQL` can break Django's transaction management and prevent proper rollback on failure.</violation>
</file>
```

</details>

<sub>Reply with feedback, questions, or to request a fix. Tag `@cubic-dev-ai` to re-run a review.</sub>

## Pull request overview

This PR eliminates the need to create separate `DataWarehouseCredential` records for each table from managed warehouse sources. Instead of storing millions of credential rows that must be updated whenever S3 credentials rotate, managed sources now use environment variable credentials by default, while self-managed sources continue to use individual credential records.

Key changes:
- Modified the `DataWarehouseTable` model to make credential references nullable and added conditional checks throughout
- Created migration 0013 to delete credentials for tables with `external_data_source_id` and set their `credential_id` to NULL
- Updated `build_function_call` in s3_table.py to use default credentials when none are provided

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| products/data_warehouse/backend/migrations/test/test_migration_0013.py | Comprehensive test suite for migration covering various credential and table scenarios |
| products/data_warehouse/backend/migrations/max_migration.txt | Updated to reference the new migration 0013 |
| products/data_warehouse/backend/migrations/0013_credential_deletion.py | SQL migration that removes credentials for managed sources and nullifies their references |
| posthog/temporal/data_imports/pipelines/pipeline_sync.py | Removed credential creation logic for synced tables |
| posthog/hogql/database/s3_table.py | Simplified credential handling to use default env vars when credentials are not provided, removed hotfix code |
</details>

body: ## Problem A [ticket](https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/46218) from a user highlighted a poor onboarding experience from PostHog AI, namely: * long prompt to agent * ai consent modal popped-up * submitted approval * re-auth triggered * lost prompt **Bonus**: submit button prior to consent been approved wasn't clickable, the user could only send the prompt (and thus init onboarding via the `return` key) https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/74b97469-5107-4a5d-be09-f73d2a42c6f4 (triggered re-auth manually since oauth flow cannot be replicated locally, same high-level flow tough) ## Changes * persist pending prompt to sessionStorage when user submits but consent isn't (yet) accepted * restore prompt on page load after redirect completes * make submit button clickable during "Pending approval" state so it triggers consent flow (same as pressing return key) ## How did you test this code? - [x] manual tests

title: fix(ci): use inline script metadata for grimp dependency
body:
**Alternative to #44302** - simpler fix for the missing grimp dependency.

**Problem**
The CI workflow `container-images-cd.yml` calls `python3 bin/find_python_dependencies.py` but grimp isn't installed at that point.

**Solution**
Use PEP 723 inline script metadata (same pattern as `bin/hobby-ci.py`):

```python
#!/usr/bin/env -S uv run --script
# /// script
# requires-python = ">=3.12"
# dependencies = [
#     "grimp==3.13",
# ]
# ///
```

When you run `uv run bin/find_python_dependencies.py`, uv reads the inline metadata and installs dependencies automatically.

**Why this over #44302**
- No separate `bin/pyproject.toml` (158-line lockfile)
- No pytest.ini changes or `--ignore=bin` workarounds
- Follows existing pattern (`bin/hobby-ci.py` does exactly this)
- Script is self-contained
- 2 files changed vs 9 files

**Test**
```
$ uv run bin/find_python_dependencies.py posthog.temporal.subscriptions --check-changes "posthog/utils.py"
Installed 2 packages in 3ms
{"affected": true, "matching_files": ["posthog/utils.py"]}

$ pytest bin/test/test_find_python_dependencies.py -v
47 passed in 0.30s
```
author: webjunkie

I'll remember it for next time!
2 files reviewed, 1 comment [Edit Code Review Agent Settings](https://app.greptile.com/review/github) | [Greptile](https://greptile.com?utm_source=greptile_expert&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=code_reviews&utm_content=posthog_44324)

## Pull request overview This PR fixes a CI issue where the `grimp` dependency was missing when executing `bin/find_python_dependencies.py` in the `container-images-cd.yml` workflow. The solution adopts PEP 723 inline script metadata to make the script self-contained, following the existing pattern used in `bin/hobby-ci.py`. **Key Changes:** - Added PEP 723 inline metadata to `bin/find_python_dependencies.py` to declare dependencies - Updated CI workflow to use `uv run` instead of `python3` for executing the script - Added `setup-uv` action installation step in the workflow ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 1 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `bin/find_python_dependencies.py` | Added PEP 723 inline script metadata header with shebang, Python version requirement (>=3.12), and grimp dependency declaration | | `.github/workflows/container-images-cd.yml` | Added `setup-uv` action step and updated script invocation from `python3` to `uv run` for automatic dependency management | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

fix(ci): use inline script metadata for grimp dependency

Use PEP 723 inline script metadata to declare grimp dependency directly
in find_python_dependencies.py. This follows the existing pattern used
by bin/hobby-ci.py and avoids creating a separate bin/pyproject.toml.

uv automatically reads the inline metadata and installs dependencies
when running the script, making it self-contained without additional
lockfiles or pytest.ini changes.

title: feat(zen-mode): persist zen mode
body:
## Problem

The Zen Mode setting in the navigation logic is not persisted between sessions, causing users to lose their preferred view mode when refreshing or returning to the app.

## Changes

Added persistence to the Zen Mode state in the navigation logic by adding the `{ persist: true }` option to the `zenMode` reducer. This ensures that a user's preference for Zen Mode is remembered across browser sessions.

## How did you test this code?

1. Enabled Zen Mode in the application
2. Refreshed the page and verified that Zen Mode remained enabled
3. Disabled Zen Mode
4. Closed the browser and reopened the application
5. Verified that Zen Mode remained disabled as expected

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: jonmcwest

title: feat(ph-ai): error tracking tool explain
body:
## Problem

PostHog AI's error tracking integration was limited and lacked proper context awareness. When users asked about explaining the errors the user was visiting, we were failing. Even the `MaxTool` we had for `Explain This` was not working.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd4c7ffa-c4b1-4070-8638-06d656011faa

This meant users had to manually navigate to error tracking pages and couldn't leverage PostHog AI for error investigation workflows E2E.

## Changes

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9a0a0cea-34be-4d10-9ad8-670167bf82b3

(this shows the E2E work after all 3 stacked PRs are combined)

* Correctly injecting current issue metadata into agent context, now the "Explain issue" workflow correctly works as expected.
* Integrate error tracking into `read_data` tool for stack trace retrieval
* Removed contextual tool as discussed in the standup (_02/01/26_) so that we don't go back to blackboxing of tool usage, avoiding LLM-within-LLM


## How did you test this code?

- [x] unit tests
- [x] manual tests

**Note**: Work is splitted into three stacked PRs:

1. https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/44050
2. https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/44055 (this one)
3. https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/44057

All the context related functions should be in an `ErrorIssueContext` class in the context folder.
Approving, just one change to be made before merging, to align frontend error tracking issue context with how the other entities are handled. Great job!

title: feat(navigation): add Zen mode and shortcuts
body:
## Problem

Sometimes the surrounding UI chrome can be a little noisy & distracting.

A "zen mode" would allow users to hide navigation and focus solely on the content they're working with.

## Changes

(This is available across the whole platform)

![2026-01-05 13 22 17](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e8665353-64ad-4e61-84ef-b390c80cae80)
![2026-01-05 13 27 51](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e12ba6a6-d1d7-4af4-99dc-5f0f73c90e3b)
![2026-01-05 13 29 11](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d3e68cd5-0df7-430f-9e3a-7582a56011e9)

- Added a new "zen mode" to the navigation system that hides most UI elements (similar to `minimal`)
- Implemented a keyboard shortcut (`⌘⌥Z`) to toggle zen mode
- Added a zen mode toggle button in the account menu
- Added an exit button when in zen mode
- Modified the navigation layout to support the zen mode view

## How did you test this code?

- Tested toggling zen mode via keyboard shortcut and UI buttons
- Verified that navigation elements are properly hidden in zen mode
- Confirmed that the exit button appears correctly when in zen mode
- Tested that the main content area displays properly with appropriate padding
- Verified that zen mode state persists correctly during navigation

## Publish to changelog?

Yes
author: jonmcwest

reviews.body: **No issues found** across 7 files

## Pull request overview This PR adds a "zen mode" feature to the navigation system that allows users to hide UI chrome and focus on content. The feature can be toggled via a keyboard shortcut (⌘⌥Z) or through UI buttons. ### Key Changes - Introduced a new "zen" navigation mode that displays minimal UI elements - Added keyboard shortcut (⌘⌥Z) for quick toggling - Integrated zen mode controls in the account menu and minimal navigation bar ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `frontend/src/lib/components/AppShortcuts/shortcuts.ts` | Added zenMode keyboard shortcut definition (⌘⌥Z) | | `frontend/src/lib/components/Account/AccountMenu.tsx` | Added zen mode menu item with keyboard shortcut indicator | | `frontend/src/layout/navigation-3000/navigationLogic.tsx` | Implemented zen mode state management and mode selector logic | | `frontend/src/layout/navigation-3000/components/ZenModeButton.tsx` | Created exit button component for zen mode | | `frontend/src/layout/navigation-3000/components/MinimalNavigation.tsx` | Integrated zen mode exit button into minimal navigation | | `frontend/src/layout/navigation-3000/Navigation.tsx` | Updated rendering logic to support zen mode layout with padding | | `frontend/src/layout/GlobalShortcuts.tsx` | Registered zen mode keyboard shortcut handler |

reviews.body: **No issues found** across 1 file

Additional Comments (1)

 1. `frontend/src/queries/nodes/DataVisualization/displayLogic.ts`, line 68-81 ([link](/posthog/posthog/blob/758690d8a5353389613d8b961b2a3f86f1143367/frontend/src/queries/nodes/DataVisualization/displayLogic.ts#L68-L81)) **style:** The logic for setting goal lines from `chartSettings` is duplicated in both the `_setQuery` listener and `afterMount`. Both check for `chartSettings.goalLines` and call `actions.setGoalLines()` with the same data. According to the simplicity rules, this violates "Says everything OnceAndOnlyOnce". Consider whether both are needed, or if the `afterMount` could be removed since `_setQuery` should fire on initial load via `propsChanged`. Note: If this suggestion doesn't match your team's coding style, reply to this and let me know. I'll remember it for next time!
1 file reviewed, 1 comment [Edit Code Review Agent Settings](https://app.greptile.com/review/github) | [Greptile](https://greptile.com?utm_source=greptile_expert&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=code_reviews&utm_content=posthog_44221)

## Pull request overview This PR fixes a race condition where goal lines configured in chart settings were visible on dashboards but failed to appear in the SQL editor's insight editor view. The issue occurred because goal lines were being set before the query data finished loading. **Key Changes:** - Added a listener for the `_setQuery` action to set goal lines after data loads - Connected to the `_setQuery` action from dataVisualizationLogic to respond to query updates
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **frontend/src/queries/nodes/DataVisualization/displayLogic.ts:81** * The logic to set goal lines from chart settings now exists in both the `_setQuery` listener (lines 69-73) and the `afterMount` hook (lines 75-81). This duplication means goal lines will be set twice on mount. Consider removing the `afterMount` implementation since the `_setQuery` listener will handle both the initial mount (via propsChanged) and subsequent updates. ``` afterMount(({ values, actions }) => { const chartSettings = values.query.chartSettings if (chartSettings?.goalLines) { actions.setGoalLines(chartSettings.goalLines) } }), ```
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message: fix: race condition loading goal lines in the SQL editor's insight editor
